meta.title: flock — an AI dev team you drive from Telegram
meta.description: flock runs a five-role Claude Code team — planner, coder, tester, reviewer, arbiter. Describe a feature in chat, get a ready pull request. Open source, Go, self-hosted.

hero.title: An AI dev team you drive from Telegram
hero.subtitle: flock runs a five-role Claude Code team — planner, coder, tester, reviewer, arbiter. Describe a feature in chat, get a ready PR. Every chat gets its own isolated workspace.

demo.title: Chat → plan → code → tests → review → PR
demo.text: You write a task in plain language. flock plans, implements on a branch, runs tests, reviews with line-anchored comments and opens a pull request.

how.title: Five roles, one pipeline
how.planner: Planner — turns the request into a spec with acceptance criteria.
how.coder: Coder — implements on a branch; for bugs, writes the failing test first.
how.tester: Tester — runs the affected suite plus regression.
how.reviewer: Reviewer — line-anchored inline comments right in the PR.
how.arbiter: Arbiter — governs the loops and rules: ready to merge, or escalate.

features.title: What's inside
features.isolation_text: Every Telegram chat gets its own workspace — projects never collide.
features.parallel_text: Multiple chats run at once, capped by config.
features.git_text: GitHub, GitLab, Gitea. PR polling catches review comments without inbound webhooks.
features.voice_text: Optional: Voxtral, Whisper or local STT — assign tasks by voice.
features.auth_text: Claude Pro/Max subscription or an Anthropic API key — no per-token billing.
features.docker_text: ghcr.io/duckbugio/flock-telegram — one compose and you're live.

paths.title: Two ways to run it
paths.self_text: Free and open. Your server, your Claude, full control.
paths.managed_text: We host flock on a dedicated VPS and handle the infrastructure.

quickstart.title: Up in a couple of commands
quickstart.step2: Set your Telegram bot token and Claude access.
quickstart.step3: Bring it up with docker compose — the bot's online.

oss.title: Fully open source
oss.text: Code, image and roles — all in the open. Fork it, audit it, contribute.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.a1: Yes. flock is open source. You only pay for your own Claude and your own server.
faq.q2: How is it different from plain Claude Code?
faq.a2: flock is a role-based team with review and an arbiter on top of Claude Code, driven from Telegram, with per-chat isolation.
faq.q3: Do I need my own server?
faq.a3: For self-hosting, yes. If you'd rather not, take managed hosting on roost.
faq.a4: Claude via a Pro/Max subscription or the Anthropic API.

final.title: Put your flock to work
final.subtitle: Self-host in minutes — or let roost host it.

footer.tagline: flock — an AI dev team in Telegram. Part of DuckBug.
